nodes.context: Armed Chinese robbers and bandits operating in eastern Russia-China borderland, southeastern Siberia, Russian Far East, and Manchuria during late 19th to early 20th century.
nodes.time_period: second half of the 19th century and the first half of the 20th century

nodes.context: Japanese military force that recruited Honghuzi as guerrillas during the Russo-Japanese War.
Chinese anti-foreign, anti-colonial movement; Honghuzi were also Boxers.
Alliance of eight nations that fought against the Boxer Rebellion.
Army of Honghuzi commanded by Zhang Zuolin, allied with Japan during the Russo-Japanese War.
Armed Chinese robbers and bandits operating in eastern Russia-China borderland, later recruited as guerrillas by Japanese.
nodes.time_period: second half of the 19th century and the first half of the 20th century

nodes.context: Japanese military force that recruited Honghuzi as guerrillas during the Russo-Japanese War.

edges.evidence_quote: Groups of honghuzi were recruited as guerrillas by the Imperial Japanese Army during the Russo-Japanese War of 1904-1905 into chunchu sabotage units.
edges.context: Honghuzi were recruited as guerrillas by the Imperial Japanese Army.

edges.evidence_quote: Louis Livingston Seaman wrote about an incident in which the Honghuzi, who were also Boxers, ambushed, tortured and executed an Eight-Nation Alliance force consisting of Sikhs
edges.context: Honghuzi were also Boxers, indicating they were part of the same movement.

edges.evidence_quote: The Honghuzi participated in the Boxer Rebellion against the Eight-Nation Alliance
edges.context: Honghuzi fought against the Eight-Nation Alliance during the Boxer Rebellion.

edges.evidence_quote: Zhang Zuolin (Chung Tsor Lin) commanded a large army of Honghuzi, who were allied to the Japanese during the war.
edges.context: Zhang Zuolin commanded a large army of Honghuzi, indicating they were part of his forces.

edges.evidence_quote: Groups of honghuzi were recruited as guerrillas by the Imperial Japanese Army during the Russo-Japanese War of 1904-1905 into chunchu (チュンチュ) sabotage units.
edges.context: Honghuzi were recruited by the Imperial Japanese Army as guerrillas.
